LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LOUIS ROESCH COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT VACATES THE TENTATIVE RULING AND THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE FIFTH, SEVENTH AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND WITHIN TEN DAYS. PLAINTIFF WILL BE ALLOWED TO ADD A DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION. MR. CORDES IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LOUIS ROESCH COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIFTH, SEVENTH AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF MAY ADD A DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION. THE ALLEGED ORAL REPRESENTATIONS BY DAVOS ARE SUBJECT TO THE PAROLE EVIDENCE RULE: C.C.P. SECTION 1856 (A). THE DECISION IN BANK OF AMERICA V PENDERGRASS (1935) 4 CAL 2D. 258, 263 IS CONTROLLING. THE ALLEGED PROMISE IS DIRECTLY AT VARIANCE WITH THE PARTIES' WRITTEN AGREEMENT AND THE FRAUD IS BARRED BY THE PAROLE EVIDENCE RULE. THE CO-OPERATION CLAUSE IS NOT SUSCEPTIBLE TO THE INTERPRETATION THAT DEFENDANT MUST CONTINUE TO EXTEND THE CLOSING DATE. NO BREACH OF CONTRACT HAS BEEN ALLEGED. THERE IS NO CLAIM FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE BECAUSE THERE IS NO BREACH OF CONTRACT. THE REFORMATION CLAIM IS DEPENDENT ON THE FRAUD CLAIM, WHICH FAILS. THE UNJUST ENRICHMENT AND COMMON COUNT CLAIMS FAIL BECAUSE THE PARTIES' CONTRACT IS ENFORCEABLE AND NOT VITIATED BY THE ALLEGED FRAUD: THE ENFORCEABLE CONTRACT BARS THE CLAIM FOR PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL. PLAINTIFF HAS NO BASIS FOR THE CONSTRUCTIVE TRUST CLAIM. MR. CORDES IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
MINI-MINUTES FOR SEP-06-2007 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LOUIS ROESCH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY AJUDICATION. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-06-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LOUIS ROESCH COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: JOSEPH M. BREALL, ESQUIRE (BREALL & BREALL, 415.345.0545) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF (NOT NAMED) IS ALSO PRESENT. CHUCK CORDES, ESQUIRE (HANSON BRIDGETT, 415.777.3200) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIFTH, SEVENTH AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF MAY ADD A DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION. THE ALLEGED ORAL REPRESENTATIONS BY DAVOS ARE SUBJECT TO THE PAROLE EVIDENCE RULE: C.C.P. SECTION 1856 (A). THE DECISION IN BANK OF AMERICA V PENDERGRASS (1935) 4 CAL 2d. 258, 263 IS CONTROLLING. THE ALLEGED PROMISE IS DIRECTLY AT VARIANCE WITH THE PARTIES' WRITTEN AGREEMENT AND THE FRAUD IS BARRED BY THE PAROLE EVIDENCE RULE. THE CO-OPERATION CLAUSE IS NOT SUSCEPTIBLE TO THE INTERPRETATION THAT DEFENDANT MUST CONTINUE TO EXTEND THE CLOSING DATE. NO BREACH OF CONTRACT HAS BEEN ALLEGED. THERE IS NO CLAIM FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE BECAUSE THERE
IS NO BREACH OF CONTRACT. THE REFORMATION CLAIM IS DEPENDENT ON THE FRAUD CLAIM, WHICH FAILS. THE UNJUST ENRICHMENT AND COMMON COUNT CLAIMS FAIL BECAUSE THE PARTIES' CONTRACT IS ENFORCEABLE AND NOT VITIATED BY THE ALLEGED FRAUD: THE ENFORCEABLE CONTRACT BARS THE CLAIM FOR PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL. PLAINTIFF HAS NO BASIS FOR THE CONSTRUCTIVE TRUST CLAIM. MR. CORDES IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/MV-X) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
